questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: For a catchment of $20\,\text{ha}$, runoff coefficient $0.6$ and rainfall intensity $50\,\text{mm/hr}$, the peak runoff (rational method, $Q=CIA/360$) is _____ m³/s.

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: For a catchment of $36\,\text{ha}$, runoff coefficient $0.5$ and rainfall intensity $40\,\text{mm/hr}$, the peak runoff (rational method, $Q=CIA/360$) is _____ m³/s.

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: For a base period of $120\,\text{days}$ and a duty of $800\,\text{ha/cumec}$, the delta is _____ m.

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: For a base period of $100\,\text{days}$ and a duty of $1000\,\text{ha/cumec}$, the delta is _____ m.

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: A storm produces $80\,\text{mm}$ of rainfall; abstractions (losses) total $30\,\text{mm}$. The direct runoff depth is _____ mm.

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: A unit hydrograph represents the direct runoff hydrograph resulting from $1\,\text{cm}$ of effective rainfall occurring
options: uniformly over the catchment in unit time
questions.solution:
**Concept.** UH = DRH from 1 cm effective rain uniformly in unit duration.

**Working.**
Uniform, unit time.

**Answer.** Uniformly in unit time

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: The discharge from a fully-penetrating well in a confined aquifer (Thiem equation) is proportional to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Thiem: $Q\propto T\,(s)$ for given radii.

**Working.**
Drawdown × transmissivity.

**Answer.** Drawdown and transmissivity

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: The combined loss of water by evaporation from soil and transpiration from plants is called

questions.subject: Hydrology & Irrigation
questions.stem: Which quantities directly increase the depth of irrigation water required?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** More consumptive use and losses raise requirement; higher efficiency and effective rainfall reduce it.

**Working.**
Options 1 and 2.

**Answer.** Consumptive use & conveyance losses
